title: pelargonium × lividum
intro: Pelargonium × lividum, a species of flowering plant in the geranium family, is a hybrid of Pelargonium linifolium and Pelargonium peltatum, known for its distinctive purple flowers and silvery-green leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems. The health benefits of Pelargonium × lividum include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and allergies. Herbal preparations of Pelargonium × lividum, such as teas and tinctures, are often used to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
